business.headline: LPG shortage shuts 20% of Mumbai hotels and restaurants: AssociationThe association warned that up to 50% of hotels in the city could shut down within the next two days if the gas supply situation does not improve.

Stock markets sink worldwide as oil prices briefly spike close to $120 per barrelThe S&P 500 fell 1.3 per cent, coming off its worst week since October. The Dow Jones Industrial Average was down 721 points, or 1.5 per cent, as of 9:35 am Eastern time, and the Nasdaq composite was 1.2 per cent lower. That followed even worse losses in European and Asian stock markets.

G7 nations hold off on emergency oil stock release as Gulf crisis lifts pricesThe bloc of the world's seven most powerful economies said it is prepared to act to stabilise oil markets if needed but believes the situation has not yet reached a stage requiring coordinated measures such as a joint release of strategic petroleum stocks.

Internet reacts to viral claimA QR code printed on a Class 12 mathematics paper of the Central Board of Secondary Education has sparked a viral debate online. Some students claim that scanning the code led them to the song 'Never Gonna Give You Up' by Rick Astley, a classic internet "rickroll." While screenshots and reactions have circulated widely on Reddit and social media, it remains unclear whether all exam papers carried the same link.

Did AI play a role in US strikes that killed 165 schoolgirls in Iran?The deaths of over 165 schoolgirls in Iran's Minab has sparked a debate over AI's deployment in warfare. Live: UAE air defences intercept Iranian missiles, dronesUS-Israel Attacks Iran Live Updates: The Middle East war entered its 11th day with a dramatic escalation over global oil supplies. Iran's Revolutionary Guards warned they would not allow "one litre of oil" to leave the region if US and Israeli strikes continue, raising fears of a wider energy shock. US President Donald Trump responded with a stark threat, saying Washington would hit Iran "twenty times harder" if it blocked tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, which carries about a fifth of the world's oil. Markets have been rattled as tankers remain stranded and producers halt pumping, while Iran's security establishment appears to be rallying behind new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei amid signs the conflict could stretch longer than expected. Follow indiatoday.in for the latest updates on the Middle East crisis.

NCERT apologises over controversial judiciary chapter, withdraws Class 8 bookThe National Council of Educational Research and Training, NCERT, withdraws its Class 8 textbook 'Exploring Society: India and Beyond – Part II' and issues a public apology over a judiciary-related chapter.
